LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. AT THE CONCLUSION OF ARGUMENT, THE COURT MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT NEW WORLD ENVIRONMENTAL, INC'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED, WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND, AS TO DEFENDANT NEW WORLD ENVIRONMENTAL, INC. DBA NEW WORLD TECHNOLOGY, INC. ("DEFENDANT NEW WORLD") DEMURRER TO THE FIRST AND SECOND CAUSES OF ACTION ON GROUNDS OF UNCERTAINTY. (CCP 430.10(F).) AS CURRENTLY PLED, THE GENERAL ALLEGATIONS OF EACH PLAINTIFF'S EMPLOYMENT RELATIONSHIP WITH THE RESPECTIVE DEFENDANTS MAKE THE FAC UNCERTAIN. (SEE FAC 14 - 17.) PLAINTIFFS ARE GIVEN LEAVE TO AMEND TO CLARIFY WHICH CODEFENDANT IS BEING SUED AND FACTS TO SUPPORT A CLAIM THAT NEW WORLD RETALIATED AGAINST JAHR. THE DEMURRERS TO THE FIRST AND SECOND CAUSES OF ACTION FOR FAILURE TO STATE FACTS SUFFICIENT TO STATE A CAUSE OF ACTION (CCP 430.10(E)) ARE SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND ON THE SAME GROUND. SUSTAINED, WITH LEAVE TO AMEND, AS TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION FOR LACK OF JURISDICTION (CCP 430.10(A), AND FOR UNCERTAINTY AND FAILURE TO STATE FACTS SUFFICIENT TO STATE A CAUSE OF ACTION FOR THE REASONS SET FORTH ABOVE. SUSTAINED AS TO NEW WORLD ENVIRONMENTAL INC. WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION, BASED ON PLAINTIFFS' CONCESSION THAT THIS CAUSE OF ACTION IS NOT PLED AGAINST DEFENDANT NEW WORLD. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

Atty. DAVID J. MARSH of Wilson, Elser, Moskowirz, Edelman & Dicker, LLP, Tel No. (415) 433-0990 appearing for Defendant New World Environmental, Inc. dba New World Technology, Inc.

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT NEW WORLD ENVIRONMENTAL, INC. DBA NEW WORLD TECHNOLOGY, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR ADJUDICATION is denied. The federal enclave doctrine applies where the pertinent events giving rise to the plaintiff's claims occur on a federal enclave. The analytical key is where the work occurred, not where the employer made the decision to terminate. See Lockart v. MVM, Inc. (2009) 175 Cal.App.4th 1452, 1459. Here, pars. 15-21 of plaintiff's declaration create a triable issue of material fact regarding whether the pertinent events that gave rise to plaintiff's claims occurred on a federal enclave. Only part of Hunters Point is a federal enclave. Plaintiff states that based on defendant's map, the dangerous remediation work that he complained about and much of plaintiff's other work occurred outside of the enclave. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Dawn Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)

Motion To Stay
Off Calendar Jan-23-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPERANCES: Atty. JOHN HOUSTON SCOTT of the Scott Law Firm, Tel No. (415) 561-9600 appearing for the Plaintiffs;
Atty. MARIE TRIMBLE HOLVICK of Gordon & Rees, LLP, Tel No. (415) 986-5900 appearing for Defendants Aleut World Solutions and Bering Sea Environmental, LLC;
Atty. RONALD S. BUSHNER of Wilson, Elser, Moskowitz, Edelman * Dicker, LLP, Tel No. (415) 625-9380 appearing for Defendant New World Environmental, Inc.;
Atty. TIMOTHY J. MURPHY of Fisher & Phillips, Tel No. (415) 490-9011 appearing for Defendant Tetra Tech EC, Inc.

RULING - The Court conducted a closed hearing. Argument presented by counsel. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS ALEUT WORLD SOLUTIONS AND BERING SEA ENVIRONMENTAL, LLC'S Motion To Stay This Action is denied. Defendants fail to show that the federal action and state action necessarily involve the same issues. The focus of the federal action is whether defendants submitted false claims to the U.S. Gov't. The focus of this action is whether plaintiffs engaged in protected activity and were subsequently terminated because of that protected activity. It appears that plaintiffs complained about safety issues and were not necessarily complaining about fraudulent billing. Moreover, a blanket stay would be overbroad. The trial judge will be in an ideal position to determine what questions or evidence implicate the defendants' Fifth Amendment rights. It is the court's understanding that the defendants have already been deposed and did not raise any Fifth Amendment concerns at their depositions. THE COURT ORDERED THAT THE COURT REPORTER'S TRANSCRIPT OF TODAY'S HEARING MAY NOT BE OBTAINED WITHOUT AN ORDER FROM THE COURT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: A. James Robertson, II; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Dawn Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/AJR)

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the court modified its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT NEW WORLD ENVIRONMENTAL, INC's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is Sustained, with 10 days leave to amend, as to Defendant New World Environmental, Inc. dba New World Technology, Inc. ("Defendant New World") demurrer to the first and second causes of action on grounds of uncertainty. (CCP 430.10(f).) As currently pled, the general allegations of each Plaintiff's employment relationship with the respective Defendants make the FAC uncertain. (See FAC 14 - 17.) Plaintiffs are given leave to amend to clarify which codefendant is being sued and facts to support a claim that New World retaliated against Jahr. The demurrers to the first and second causes of action for failure to state facts sufficient to state a cause of action (CCP 430.10(e)) are sustained with leave to amend on the same ground. Sustained, with leave to amend, as to the third cause of action for lack of jurisdiction (CCP 430.10(a), and for uncertainty and failure to state facts sufficient to state a cause of action for the reasons set forth above. Sustained as to New World Environmental Inc. without leave to amend as to the fourth cause of action, based on Plaintiffs' concession that this cause of action is not pled against Defendant New World.
